summary: Connect options 'connect' is ignored.
description: When creating a new Mongo instance, when setting the connect option to influence automatic connecting, the setting is ignored when it's `false`. Result is, it's always connecting. I have another version of the pecl extension running which is 1.0.11 which acts normally on the same code: http://snipr.it/~qr

summary: The wrong connection is sometimes picked when there are two connections open
It'd be nice if mongo was playing nicely with pcntl php extension
description:
Currently using experimental (but stable and quite reliable) pcntl extension with mongo doesn't really work well. 
We're getting all kinds of weird exceptions like:

> bad response length: 1852140903, max: 67108864, did the db assert?
> couldn't find a response

It'd be great if you could make them (driver and pcntl) play nicely together.

I'm attaching a script that reproduces these exceptions but collection must be big enough (50000 rows+).
Thanks!

summary: Uncaught exception 'MongoCursorException' with message 'couldn't send query: ' from PHP
description:
I'm sporadically getting this error on our development environment from PHP code:

  Uncaught exception 'MongoCursorException' with message 'couldn't send query: ' 

 
The code is really simple - it's just doing a findOne query on the shard key.

  $cursor = $customer_collection->findOne(
      array("EmailAddress" => $emailaddress),
      $return_cols
  );


This generally will happen several times in a row and then clears up for a short while.

Discussion on google groups: https://groups.google.com/forum/?fromgroups#!topic/mongodb-user/IJ1n_Xt_al8

summary: The driver crashes after having copied a server definition to resolve authenticated connections
Add support for w=0 and make sure append_getlasterror sets the correct flags
mongo::connect() should return a bool value
description:
The Mongo::connect() function previously returned a bool value, there's some portion of code out there that's expecting this to continue.

The latest build from Github returns nothing on connect, and throws an exception on success.

summary: Problems with auth-switch and wrong credentials
description:
Switching away from an authed-db to a new one, without supplying proper credentials segfaults the driver:

<?php
$m = new Mongo( 'mongodb://user:user@whisky:27017/test' );

$db = $m->test2; ← new database name (test2)
$db->authenticate('user2', 'user2' ); ← wrong credentials
$collection = $db->collection;
$collection->findOne(); ← segfault
?>

description:
Filtering on items on an aggregation call does not work when item is in an array.
json: 1
data: [ {y:1,x:'x'},{y:1,x:'x'} ]
so, if I want to match on data.x = x it does not work sample code below.

in php

summary: Host is ignored on Mongo::__construct()
mongo.is_master_interval unused
description:
The ismaster interval isn't configurable, even though the INI setting exists.
Also, the ini setting should be called "ismaster_interval".

summary: mongo.ping_interval unused
description: The mongo.ping_interval INI setting isn't actually used anywhere :(

summary: Change readPreference constants to be strings
The third param in MongoCollection::aggregate is name $... which is impossible to mock.
description: Because the param is named "..." which is an invalid name in php itself, it's now impossible to mock that method. There is no way to write it and therefor will always raise an error for not extending properly.

summary: Incorrect constant values for MongoClient::RP_*
description: The constants changed just before 1.3.0 and are now strings

summary: Review the install guide
description: The install guide is really old and looks awful.

summary: Update Changelog with 1.3. changes
description: The changelog in the documentations should be updated to reflect the major changes in 1.3

summary: Incorrect method declaration
description:
Inheriting class MongoCollection couses an error:
Strict standards: Declaration of SdfDb_AbstractionMongoCollection::batchInsert() should be compatible with that of MongoCollection::batchInsert()

Let`s take a look to method declaration in documenation http://php.net/manual/en/mongocollection.batchinsert.php :
public mixed MongoCollection::batchInsert ( array $a [, array $options = array() ] )

If we use a simple Reflection to check actual declaration:
$reflection = new ReflectionClass( 'MongoCollection' );
foreach ( $reflection->getMethod('batchInsert')->getParameters() as $param ) {
	var_dump( $param->getName(), $param->isArray(), $param->isOptional() );
}

We`ll see:

string 'array_of_documents' (length=18)
boolean false
boolean false
string 'array_of_options' (length=16)
boolean false
boolean true

But correct output is:

string 'array_of_documents' (length=18)
boolean true
boolean false
string 'array_of_options' (length=16)
boolean true
boolean true

summary: Document MongoClient::getConnections()
Use descending date order for changelog entries
description:
http://uk3.php.net/manual/en/changelog.mongo.php lists several changelogs for 1.3.0 - which doesn't make whole lot of sense as I am sure most of these items are in the current release.

For example the 1.3.0 changelog entry for http://uk3.php.net/manual/en/mongocollection.insert.php says the 2nd parameter was a boolean before 1.3.0, while the entry above it 1.2.0 says that the parameter has been an array for a while?

summary: Restructure manual
description: The manual follows no normal PHP structure and is really confusing to navigate.

summary: Update and remove options have reversed keys in the description
description: http://www.php.net/manual/en/mongocollection.findandmodify.php#refsect1-mongocollection.findandmodify-parameters update and remove description have from names. Remove has update and visa versa.

summary: Document WriteConcerns
Replace slave language in MongoClient::getHosts() documentation with read preferences
Document that MongoCollection::save() may set _id
slaveOk option not documented in Mongo.__construct()
description: The slaveOk option is not documented in the Mongo constructor.

summary: Segfault while running phpunit
Segfaults in Mongo PHP driver 1.3RC3
description:
As suggested by Derick on groups (https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/mongodb-user/3h5hE8uJs2M), I'm posting our bug to jira.


Basically, we get segfaults when running complex queries or multiple queries in sequence.

I've attached the outputs of gdb 'bt full' and valgrind. If you need a more detailed valgrind, I would glad to produce it.

I'll also try to find a sequence of queries that segfaults that I can share here.


Thanks !!

summary: Segfault occurs on some of the queries
mongo.allow_persistent doesn't exist anymore
description: The mongo.allow_persistent is still declared and document but no longer used.

summary: Remove mongo.no_id
description:
There is a totally weirdo undocumented INI option called "mongo.no_id" which seemingly removes the $MongoID->$id property for unknown reason.

This option should be removed rather then documented.

summary: No longer possible to get field information from $cursor->info()
description:
As of 1.3.0 it is no longer possible to retrieve information about a cursor before it has been created on a server.
This means we can no longer dump out the currently applied flags, or limits or anything before executing the query.

summary: Change in behaviour for return value of unsafe writes
description:
Meta ticket for https://bugs.php.net/bug.php?id=63050

Description:
------------
---
From manual page: http://www.php.net/mongo.writes
---

In version 1.2.x of the driver, when performing a write operation with the "safe 
=> false" (unsafe) flag set, it would return a boolean value.
In 1.3.0 (beta2) it appears that this is no longer the case, as these operations 
have no return value (null).
While this makes sense - since we won't really know if the operation succeeded - 
this will break applications which expect that boolean return value.

Therefore this change in behaviour should be noted on the documentation page for 
mongo write operations.

summary: When replicaSet is set, Mongo->connected is always TRUE
description: https://bugs.php.net/bug.php?id=60508

summary: Exception on write concern timeouts should be MongoCursorTimeoutException
description: We currently throw MongoCursorException, not MongoCursorTimeoutException when the primary hits the wtimeout limit.

summary: Handle empty database name better
Segfault when no candidate servers found
MongoGridFS::get() only accepts MongoId arguments
PHP Driver failure on OSX
description:
1. Checked out and built from git master branch for the driver
2. Modified php.ini accordingly
3. php -m shows mongo installed
4. Run the following:
<?php
$m = new MongoClient();
$db = $m->selectDB('test');
?>
5. Results in:
dyld: lazy symbol binding failed: Symbol not found: _strndup
  Referenced from: /usr/lib/php/extensions/no-debug-non-zts-20100525/mongo.so
  Expected in: flat namespace

dyld: Symbol not found: _strndup
  Referenced from: /usr/lib/php/extensions/no-debug-non-zts-20100525/mongo.so
  Expected in: flat namespace

Trace/BPT trap

summary: Connection failure
description:
I tried to connect to a database called 'test' using this syntax:
$m = new MongoClient("mongodb://localhost", array("db" => "test"));

This is the result:
php(30728) malloc: *** mmap(size=18446744069401817088) failed (error code=12)
*** error: can't allocate region
*** set a breakpoint in malloc_error_break to debug
Segmentation fault

The connection works fine using:
$m = new MongoClient();
$db = $m->selectDB('test');

Looking at the docs I think the first syntax _should_ work even if I have not started mongo with --auth (which I haven't)

summary: MINKEY is not deserialized properly when reading back from MongoD (on 32bit platforms)
MongoGridFS::get() requires MongoID but documentation claims 'mixed' is acceptable
description:
The documenation for MongoGridFS::get() shows "mixed $id" as the argument which implies that the string value of a MongoID may be acceptable.  However, only instances of MongoID are accepted.

Fixes:
Update documentation to show MongoGridFS::get(MongoID $id) method signature and maybe the parameters text to also indicate a MongoID is expected.

or

Update the function to accept the string form of the MongoID.

Rated trivial, as it may simply be a documentation issue, but since it's a "convenience" function, it might as well accept the string, no?  find() and findOne() expect you to type-match your query so it makes sense that you'd use MongoIDs there.

summary: MongoGridFS::storeFile() insertion error without safe option
Impossible to provide a list of tagsets to the readPreferenceTags options
description:
We need to change the prototype to "readPreferenceTags" => array("dc:ny,rack:42", "dc:ny,rack:69", "dc:ny", "dc:sf", "");

Currently the value is a string and doesn't allow you to specify a list of tagset fallbacks at all.

summary: Missing support for passing the username/password in the $options array
description:
<?php

$mc = new MongoClient("localhost", array("username" => "user", "password" => "pass");

?>
We should default on the admin database when none provided.
We correctly do that when the credentials are part of the connection uri

summary: Mongo PHP driver v1.3.1/1.3.2RC1 fail to connect to mongoDB 1.6.3
description:
When attempting to connect using the 1.3.x PHP driver (tested 1.3.1 on Linux and 1.3.2RC1 on Windows) to a mongoDB v1.6.3 server, an exception is thrown:

Uncaught exception 'MongoException' with message 'size of BSON doc is 78 bytes, max is 0'

We have a large number of customers using a variety of different mongoDB versions, if it's possible to fix 1.3.x so that it supports both mongoDB 1.6.x and 2.x that would be great!
